key_insight: PHO-Agents combines structured EHR modeling with LLM-based multi-agent clinical reasoning to improve health outcome prediction and explanation quality.
clinical_relevance: This hybrid system could support more trustworthy risk stratification and decision support for patients, including those receiving cancer immunotherapy.
method: Encoded longitudinal EHR sequences into attention-based patient representations and initial prediction logits.
Converted EHR outputs into patient summaries for a data agent, then used retrieval, research, doctor, and leader agents to review clinical evidence.
Fused EHR model outputs with LLM agent analyses to generate final predictions and explanation reports.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on three real-world cohorts (AKI mortality, CKD-related AKI onset, and immune-related adverse events in cancer patients), with PHO-Agents outperforming single-agent and multi-agent LLM baselines; in AKI mortality it achieved PR-AUC 90.20 ± 2.07 versus 56.46 ± 2.98 for the best single-agent baseline.
watch_item: Promising results still need careful validation for workflow integration, calibration, and generalization across sites, populations, and clinical settings.
paper_title: PHO-Agents: A Large Language Model Powered Multi-Agent System for Predicting Health Outcomes
